review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-122-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: Cross-tenant and leak-prevention behaviors are exercised only in the secondary_workflow phase
findings.evidence: PERMISSION_MATRIX_TITLES has 22 entries and each phase takes a 20-wide window: RP02.P06.M04 uses start_index 3 (titles Search…Leak prevention) and RP02.P06.M05 uses start_index 1 (titles Permission matrix row…Denied). As a result the permission_audit_binding slice (RP02.P06.M05.S01-S20) substitutes permission_matrix_row and view_decision_binding for cross_tenant_test and leak_prevention_test, and validatePermissionKernelCp122Coverage only asserts cross_tenant_test/leak_prevention_test under the secondary_workflow prefix.
findings.recommendation: Confirm this per-unit behavior distribution matches the closeout-pack-plan intent for RP02.P06.M05.S01-S20. No boundary gap exists: cross-tenant fail-closed (cross_tenant_deny + redacted object) and leak-prevention assertions remain exercised via the secondary_workflow phase, and both phases cover search/trimming/deny. Non-blocking.

parsed_review.rationale: The diff adds a synthetic, metadata-only Risk B permission matrix workflow binding module that closes all 40 planned units (RP02.P06.M04.S03-RP02.P06.M05.S20) across two micro-phases, with first/last unit IDs, area counts (20/20), and deliverable counts (implementation 15, security_audit 9, ui 8, claude_review 2, test 6) all enforced by validatePermissionKernelCp122Coverage and the contract validation script. It inherits CP121 Risk A boundaries via the imported boundary catalog and approval-route case (inherited_boundary_case_count 10, approval_required_routing). Sensitive decisions are routed without execution: export/download and review route to review_required_routing, mutation/share/approval to approval_required_routing, and AI retrieval to blocked_ai_retrieval_boundary; evaluatePermission/trimSearchResults are read-only evaluators. Security trimming returns only the authorized resource id (d_cp122_document), omits unauthorized cross-tenant/ACL-denied rows without emitting counts, and renders only an allowlist of safe fields (privileged_note never surfaced). Audit cases stay preview-only (emitted_to_audit_ledger false), and fixtures/matrix rows carry persisted false. Every result passes through freezeNoWriteResult, which stamps synthetic_only/no_real_data true and sixteen no-write/no-leak flags false (policy mutation, audit/product/database writes, idempotency/lock persistence, rollback/retry, export/share/AI/analytics execution, approval grant, LDIP), and the validators reject any true flag. No runtime UI/API route, route registration, fs/network/db access, or HRX split appears; HRX stays embedded and handoff is fixed to CP00-123 / RP02.P06.M05.S21. Contract (v0.16), README, tests, and the validation script are internally consistent with the module. The single P3 is an intentional, non-blocking windowed-behavior observation.
